steps.title: Find the key signal in the sequence
steps.content: The word “before” sets up a critical contrast: the conjecture did something to mathematicians, and only after that did it “yield” (finally get proven). Since “yielding” implies resistance was overcome, the blank needs a word describing resistance or difficulty.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: ”Confounded” means defeated or frustrated — what a hard, unsolved conjecture does to mathematicians trying to solve it. The other three either contradict this logic or are irrelevant to mathematical difficulty. That matches choice C — C is correct.
